test cases.title: Successful user registration with valid credentials
preconditions: User is on the Registration Screen
steps: Enter a unique valid email address in the 'Email' field (e.g., 'newuser@example.com')
Enter a valid full name in the 'Full Name' field (e.g., 'John Doe')
Enter a complex password (at least 8 characters) in the 'Password' field (e.g., 'SecureP@ss1')
test cases.expectedResult: User account is created successfully, a success message is displayed, and user is redirected to the dashboard/home screen.

test cases.title: Attempt to register with an already registered email address (Real-time Validation)
preconditions: User is on the Registration Screen
An account exists with 'existing@example.com'
test cases.expectedResult: A real-time validation error message 'Email already in use' or similar is displayed below the email field.

test cases.title: Attempt to register with an invalid email format
preconditions: User is on the Registration Screen
steps: Enter an invalid email (e.g., 'invalid-email', 'user@domain') in the 'Email' field
test cases.expectedResult: An error message 'Invalid email format' or similar is displayed below the email field, and account creation is prevented.

test cases.title: Attempt to register with a password not meeting complexity requirements (e.g., < 8 characters)
preconditions: User is on the Registration Screen
steps: Enter a unique valid email address
Enter a password less than 8 characters (e.g., 'shortp')
test cases.expectedResult: An error message 'Must be at least 8 characters' is displayed below the password field, and account creation is prevented.

test cases.title: Attempt to register with empty Full Name field
preconditions: User is on the Registration Screen
steps: Enter a unique valid email address
test cases.expectedResult: An error message 'Full Name is required' or similar is displayed, and account creation is prevented.

test cases.title: Password mask/unmask functionality on Registration Screen
preconditions: User is on the Registration Screen
steps: Enter a password in the 'Password' field
Verify the password is masked (e.g., dots or asterisks)
Tap the 'Mask/Unmask' toggle icon in the password field
test cases.expectedResult: The password should be visible when the unmask icon is toggled, and masked again when the mask icon is toggled. The toggle icon state should change accordingly.

test cases.title: Network interruption during registration API call
preconditions: User is on the Registration Screen
Network connection can be controlled
test cases.expectedResult: An appropriate error message indicating network issues is displayed (e.g., 'No internet connection', 'Failed to connect'), and user can retry registration after network is restored.

test cases.title: Successful user login with valid credentials
preconditions: User is on the Login Screen
An account exists with 'user@example.com' and 'SecureP@ss1'
test cases.expectedResult: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the dashboard/home screen. Average login time is under 3 seconds.

test cases.title: Attempt to log in with incorrect password
preconditions: User is on the Login Screen
An account exists with 'user@example.com'
steps: Enter an incorrect password (e.g., 'WrongP@ss1') in the 'Password' field
test cases.expectedResult: An error message 'Invalid credentials' or similar is displayed, and user remains on the login screen.

test cases.title: Attempt to log in with non-existent email address
preconditions: User is on the Login Screen
steps: Enter a non-existent email (e.g., 'nonexistent@example.com') in the 'Email Address' field
Enter any password in the 'Password' field
test cases.expectedResult: An error message 'Invalid credentials' or similar is displayed, and user remains on the login screen.

test cases.title: Attempt to log in with empty Email Address field
preconditions: User is on the Login Screen
test cases.expectedResult: An error message 'Email address is required' or similar is displayed below the email field, and login is prevented.

test cases.title: Password mask/unmask functionality on Login Screen
preconditions: User is on the Login Screen
steps: Enter a password in the 'Password' field
Verify the password is masked (e.g., dots or asterisks)
Tap the 'Mask/Unmask' toggle icon in the password field
test cases.expectedResult: The password should be visible when the unmask icon is toggled, and masked again when the mask icon is toggled. The toggle icon state should change accordingly.

test cases.title: Network interruption during login API call
preconditions: User is on the Login Screen
Network connection can be controlled
test cases.expectedResult: An appropriate error message indicating network issues is displayed (e.g., 'No internet connection', 'Failed to connect'), and user can retry login after network is restored.

test cases.title: Successful login via Google SSO for an existing user
preconditions: User is on the Login Screen
User has an existing account linked to a Google account
Google Play Services / iOS equivalent is available
steps: Select the Google account linked to the existing user
test cases.expectedResult: User is successfully logged in via Google SSO and redirected to the dashboard/home screen.

test cases.title: Successful registration and login via Google SSO for a new user
preconditions: User is on the Login Screen
User has a Google account not yet linked to any existing application account
Google Play Services / iOS equivalent is available
test cases.expectedResult: A new user account is successfully created and linked to the Google account. User is logged in and redirected to the dashboard/home screen.

test cases.title: Attempt to login with Google SSO when email already exists via Email/Password
preconditions: User is on the Login Screen
An account exists with 'user@example.com' registered via Email/Password
User attempts to log in with Google SSO using the same 'user@example.com' Google account
test cases.expectedResult: The user should be successfully logged in, and the existing Email/Password account should be linked to the Google SSO. No duplicate account is created.

test cases.title: Cancel Google SSO flow
preconditions: User is on the Login Screen
steps: In the Google account selection/permission prompt, tap 'Cancel' or navigate back
test cases.expectedResult: The Google SSO flow is aborted, and the user remains on the Login Screen without any error messages, or an informative message about cancellation is displayed.

test cases.title: Successful 'Forgot Password' flow and password reset
preconditions: User is on the Login Screen
An account exists with 'forgotuser@example.com'
Email service is functional
steps: Access the email client and open the password reset email
Tap the reset link in the email
On the password reset page, enter a new complex password
Return to the app's Login Screen
Log in with 'forgotuser@example.com' and the new password
test cases.expectedResult: A confirmation message 'Password reset email sent' is displayed. The user receives a valid reset email. The password reset is successful, and the user can log in with the new password.

test cases.title: Request password reset for a non-existent email address
preconditions: User is on the Forgot Password input screen
test cases.expectedResult: A generic confirmation message 'If an account exists with this email, a reset link will be sent' is displayed to prevent enumeration attacks, without explicitly confirming if the email exists.

test cases.title: Attempt to use an expired password reset link
preconditions: User requested a password reset link which has now expired
steps: Open the expired password reset link
test cases.expectedResult: An error message 'This password reset link has expired' or similar is displayed, prompting the user to request a new link.

test cases.title: Attempt to reset password with a new password not meeting complexity
preconditions: User has clicked a valid password reset link and is on the 'Set New Password' screen
steps: Enter a new password less than 8 characters (e.g., 'newp')
test cases.expectedResult: An error message 'Must be at least 8 characters' is displayed, and the password reset is prevented.

test cases.title: Verify UI layout adherence to Figma design (margins, typography)
preconditions: User is on Login Screen
User is on Registration Screen
steps: Observe the screen layout, component spacing, and text styles on both Login and Registration screens.
test cases.expectedResult: The UI elements adhere to Figma specifications: 16px gutter margins, 'Inter Bold 24px' for titles, 'Inter Regular 14px' for body text.

test cases.title: Verify input field height for touch targets
preconditions: User is on Login Screen
User is on Registration Screen
steps: Inspect the height of all input fields (Email, Password, Full Name).
test cases.expectedResult: All input fields have a height of 44px, providing adequate touch targets as per Figma design.

test cases.title: Verify contrast ratio for buttons
preconditions: User is on Login Screen
User is on Registration Screen
steps: Visually inspect or use accessibility tools to check the contrast ratio of all buttons (e.g., 'Sign In', 'Create Account', 'Continue with Google') against their background.
test cases.expectedResult: All buttons meet the minimum contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for accessibility.

test cases.title: Verify 'slide-left' transition from Register to Login screen
preconditions: User is on the Registration Screen
steps: Tap the 'Already a member? Login' link
test cases.expectedResult: The screen transitions from the Registration Screen to the Login Screen with a smooth 'slide-left' animation.

test cases.title: Verify clickable links and navigation
preconditions: User is on Login Screen
User is on Registration Screen
steps: On Forgot Password screen, navigate back
On Login Screen, tap 'Don't have an account? Sign Up'
On Registration Screen, tap 'Already a member? Login'
test cases.expectedResult: All links navigate to the correct respective screens as intended by the flow.

test cases.title: App background/foreground during active authentication flow
preconditions: User is midway through an authentication flow (e.g., typing credentials, on Forgot Password screen)
steps: Navigate to the Login/Registration screen and start filling out details
Send the app to the background
Bring the app back to the foreground
test cases.expectedResult: The application should resume its state correctly, and any entered data should be preserved. The authentication flow can be continued without interruption or loss of data.

test cases.title: User remains logged in after app restart (refresh token persistence)
preconditions: User is successfully logged in to the application
steps: Successfully log in to the application
Close the application completely (kill from recent apps)
test cases.expectedResult: The user should remain logged in and be directed to the dashboard/home screen, indicating that the refresh token was successfully used to obtain new access tokens. (Expected behavior based on RFC HttpOnly refresh token)

test cases.title: Access Token expiry handling (15 min)
preconditions: User is logged in
Access token has a 15-minute expiry (RFC)
steps: Wait for more than 15 minutes (without any foreground activity/API calls that might refresh tokens)
Attempt to perform an action requiring authentication (e.g., navigate to a protected screen or make an API call)
test cases.expectedResult: The application should silently use the refresh token to obtain a new access token, allowing the user to continue their session without re-authenticating. If refresh token fails (e.g., also expired or invalid), the user should be prompted to log in again.
